assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and email address.
No Authorize.net sandbox account or API Login ID / Transaction Key exists yet.
Sandbox charging a test card is the intended first-success family; production merchant boarding is later.

developer_goal: Create an Authorize.net sandbox account, obtain API credentials, and run the Hello World first test charge.

first_success.outcome: A sandbox authCaptureTransaction succeeds and returns a transaction ID and authorization code.
first_success.observable_signal: Hello World describes charging a test card and printing the auth code and transaction ID.

route_selection.surface: Authorize.net Developer Center sandbox signup and Hello World
route_selection.rule: Prefer sandbox over production. Do not invent a winner among language samples when the Hello World presents multiple implementations.

candidates.name: Sandbox signup plus Java Hello World sample
candidates.reason: Java appears first in the preserved research path, but public Hello World evidence does not prove it is the only or shortest language route.

candidates.name: Sandbox signup plus another official language sample or API Reference try-it request
candidates.reason: API Reference and Hello World both support authenticated sandbox calls; language choice changes install and run actions.

required_path.action: Create an Authorize.net sandbox account on the official signup form.
required_path.observable_result: The sandbox account is created and Merchant Interface login becomes available.

required_path.action: Sign in to the sandbox Merchant Interface and create a new Transaction Key under API Credentials & Keys.

required_fields.label: Credential verification PIN
required_fields.notes: PIN delivery channel and timing are not specified.

required_path.observable_result: A Transaction Key is revealed for sandbox API authentication.

required_path.action: Run the selected Hello World or API Reference sample that submits an authCaptureTransaction with sandbox credentials and a test card.
required_path.observable_result: The console or response shows a successful transaction ID and authorization code.

prerequisites.description: A language runtime matching the chosen Hello World sample is required before the first charge can run locally.

external_gates.description: Requesting a new Transaction Key requires requesting and verifying a PIN before the key is shown.

unavoidable_waits.description: PIN delivery for Transaction Key generation may introduce an external wait whose channel and duration are not documented.

platform_outcomes.description: Sandbox simulates card-network responses without processing real card payments.
Successful Hello World charge returns transaction identifiers and an authorization code.

excluded.item: Production merchant account boarding and live credentials
excluded.reason: Hello World and sandbox docs keep production separate; mixing credentials returns error 13.

excluded.item: Refund, settlement, and error-condition testing guides
excluded.reason: Documented as additional sandbox capabilities after the first successful charge.

sources.title: Authorize.net Hello world
evidence_supported: First API call by charging a test card

sources.title: Common setup questions
evidence_supported: API Login ID and Transaction Key
PIN verification for new Transaction Key

uncertainties.question: What happens immediately after Create Account, including email confirmation or activation?
uncertainties.impact: Extra steps before Merchant Interface login would change the required path.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh sandbox signup through first Merchant Interface login.

uncertainties.question: How is the Transaction Key PIN delivered, and how long can it take?
uncertainties.impact: Adds an external wait and possibly a device or email interaction that cannot be counted precisely.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official detail or observed PIN delivery channel, expiry, and retry behavior.

uncertainties.question: Which Hello World language or API Reference try-it path is the canonical shortest implementation?
uncertainties.impact: Install and run actions differ by language while targeting the same charge outcome.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Human selection of one implementation surface after confirming sample credential-replacement instructions.
